name: humemai

metadata.name: humemai
metadata.summary: A Machine With Human-Like Memory Systems

documentation:
# humemai [![DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/614376180.svg)](https://zenodo.org/doi/10.5281/zenodo.10876440) [![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/humemai.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/humemai)
- Built on a cognitive architecture - Functions as the brain 🧠 of your own agent - It has human-like short-term and long-term memory - The memory is represented as a knowledge graph - A graph database (JanusGraph + Cassandra) is used for persistence and fastgraph traversal - The user does not have to know graph query languages, e.g., Gremlin, since HumemAI handles read from / write to the database - The interface of HumemAI is natural language, just like a chatbot. - This requires the Text2Graph and Graph2Text modules, which are part of HumemAI - Everything is open-sourced, including the database ## Installation The `humemai` python package can already be found in [the PyPI server](https://pypi.org/project/humemai/) ```sh pip install humemai ``` or ```sh pip install 'humemai[dev]' ``` for the development Supports python>=3.10 ## Text2Graph and Graph2Text These two modules are critical in HumemAI. At the moment, they are achieved with [LLM prompting](./humemai/prompt/), which is not ideal. They'll be replaced with Transformer and GNN based neural networks. ## Example - [`example-janus-agent.ipynb`](./examples/janus-graph-parse-text/example-janus-agent.ipynb): This Jupyter Notebook reads the Harry Potter book paragraph by paragraph and turns it into a knowledge graph. Text2Graph and Graph2Text are achieved with LLM prompting. - More to come ... ## Visualizaing Graph Use [`JanusGraph-Visualizer`](https://github.com/JanusGraph/janusgraph-visualizer) to visualize the graph. Run below: ```sh docker run --rm -d -p 3000:3000 -p 3001:3001 --name=janusgraph-visualizer --network=host janusgraph/janusgraph-visualizer:latest ``` And open `http://localhost:3001/` on your web browser ## Work in progress Currently this is a one-man job. [Click here to see the current progress](https://github.com/orgs/humemai/projects/2/). ## pdoc documentation Click on [this link](https://humemai.github.io/humemai) to see the HTML rendered docstrings ## Contributing Contributions are what make the open source community such an amazing place to be learn, inspire, and create. Any contributions you make are **greatly appreciated**. 1. Fork the Project 1. Create your Feature Branch (`git checkout -b feature/AmazingFeature`) 1. Run `make test && make style && make quality` in the root repo directory, to ensure code quality. 1. Commit your Changes (`git commit -m 'Add some AmazingFeature'`) 1. Push to the Branch (`git push origin feature/AmazingFeature`) 1. Open a Pull Request ## Authors - [Taewoon Kim](https://taewoon.kim/)

modules.name: humemai.utils
documentation: General utility functions.

functions.name: humemai.utils.is_iso8601_datetime
documentation:
Check if the given string is in ISO 8601 datetime format with seconds precision.

Args:
    value (str): The string to check.

Returns:
    bool: True if the string is a valid ISO 8601 datetime, False otherwise.

functions.name: humemai.utils.disable_logger
documentation:
Disables a specific logger or the root logger.

Args:
    logger_name (str | None): The name of the logger to disable. If None, disables
    the root logger.

functions.name: humemai.utils.parse_file_by_paragraph
documentation:
Reads a .txt file and parses its content into paragraphs.
Paragraphs are separated by a specified minimum number of consecutive newlines.

Args:
    file_path (str): Path to the .txt file.
    least_newlines (int): Minimum number of consecutive newlines to define a paragraph break.

Returns:
    list[str]: A list of strings, each representing a paragraph.

functions.name: humemai.utils.write_json
documentation:
Save a dictionary as a JSON file.

Args:
    data (dict): The dictionary to save.
    file_path (str): The path to save the JSON file.

functions.name: humemai.utils.read_json
documentation:
Load a JSON file as a dictionary.

Args:
    file_path (str): The path to the JSON file.

Returns:
    dict: The dictionary loaded from the JSON file.

modules.name: humemai.janusgraph.agent.prompt_agent
documentation: PromptAgent class using JanusGraph-Cassandra as backend.

classes.name: humemai.janusgraph.agent.prompt_agent.PromptAgent

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.agent.prompt_agent.PromptAgent.__init__
documentation:
Initialize the agent.

Args:
    warmup_seconds (int): The number of seconds to wait for the containers to
        warm up. Defaults to 10.
    remove_data_on_start (bool): Whether to remove all data from the database
        on start. Defaults to True.
    num_hops_for_working_memory (int): The number of hops to consider for the
        working memory. Defaults to 2.
    turn_on_logger (bool): Whether to turn on the logger. Defaults to True.
    llm_config (dict): The configuration for the Hugging Face pipeline.
        Defaults to {
            "model": "meta-llama/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct",
            "device": "cuda",
            "quantization": "16bit",
        }. The model, device, and quantization can be changed.
    text2graph_template (str): The template to use for text2graph. Defaults to
        "text2graph_without_properties".

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.agent.prompt_agent.PromptAgent.reset_working_memory
documentation: Reset the working memory of the agent.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.agent.prompt_agent.PromptAgent.update_working_memory
documentation: Update the working memory of the agent.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.agent.prompt_agent.PromptAgent.return_working_memory_as_dict
documentation:
Return the working memory as a dictionary.

This is necessary for generating the prompt with an LLM. It merges short-term
and (partial) long-term memories.

Returns:
    dict: The working memory as a dictionary.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.agent.prompt_agent.PromptAgent.generate_graph
documentation:
Process the input text and convert it into a knowledge graph.

Args:
    text (str): The input text to process.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.agent.prompt_agent.PromptAgent.save_as_short_term_memory
documentation:
Save the entities and relations as short-term memory.

Args:
    entities (dict): The entities to save as short-term memory.
    relations (dict): The relations to save as short-term memory.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.agent.prompt_agent.PromptAgent.save_as_long_term_memory
documentation:
Move the short-term memories to the long-term memory.

At the moment, they are all moved as "episodic". In the future, we may want to
move some of them as "semantic" memories, or even forget some of them.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.agent.prompt_agent.PromptAgent.step
documentation:
Process the input (text), convert it into a knowledge graph, and save it as
short-term memory.

Args:
    text (str): The input text to process.

Returns:
    str: HumemAI's generated text from the working memory.

Agent class using JanusGraph-Cassandra as backend.

This is the simplest agent that uses JanusGraph-Cassandra as the backend. It
does not use properties of the vertices and edges. It takes one text at a time,
converts it into a knowledge graph, and saves it as short-term memory. It then
moves the short-term memory to long-term memory. There is no forgetting mechanism
implemented in this agent. You also can't talk to the agent. It only processes
the input text and updates the memory.

modules.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.docker
documentation: Utility functions for managing JanusGraph and Cassandra containers.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.docker.start_containers
documentation:
Start JanusGraph and Cassandra containers.

Args:
    configs_dir (str): Directory containing JanusGraph and Gremlin Server
    configuration files.
    janusgraph_config (str): JanusGraph configuration file.
    gremlin_server_config (str): Gremlin Server configuration file.
    cassandra_data_dir (str): Directory for Cassandra data persistence.
    cassandra_container_name (str): Name of the Cassandra container.
    janusgraph_container_name (str): Name of the JanusGraph container.
    warmup_seconds (int): Number of seconds to wait for the containers to warm up.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.docker.stop_containers
documentation:
Stop JanusGraph and Cassandra containers.

Args:
    cassandra_container_name (str): Name of the Cassandra container.
    janusgraph_container_name (str): Name of the JanusGraph container.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.docker.remove_containers
documentation:
Remove JanusGraph and Cassandra containers.

Args:
    cassandra_container_name (str): Name of the Cassandra container.
    janusgraph_container_name (str): Name of the JanusGraph container.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.docker.copy_file_from_docker
documentation:
Copy a file from a Docker container to the host machine using docker cp.

Args:
    container_name (str): Name or ID of the container.
    source_path (str): Path to the file inside the container.
    destination_path (str): Destination path on the host machine.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.docker.copy_file_to_docker
documentation:
Copy a file from the host machine to a Docker container using docker cp.

Args:
    container_name (str): Name or ID of the container.
    source_path (str): Path to the file on the host machine.
    destination_path (str): Destination path inside the container.

modules.name: humemai.memory
documentation:
Memory class for HumemAI.

This module provides a Memory class that represents a relationship between two nodes
with an edge connecting them.

classes.name: humemai.memory.Memory

methods.name: humemai.memory.Memory.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a Memory instance.

Args:
    head_label (str): Label for the head node.
    tail_label (str): Label for the tail node.
    edge_label (str): Label for the edge between the head and tail nodes.
    head_properties (dict[str, any], optional): Properties for the head node.
        Defaults to an empty dict if not provided.
    tail_properties (dict[str, any], optional): Properties for the tail node.
        Defaults to an empty dict if not provided.
    edge_properties (dict[str, any], optional): Properties for the edge.
        Defaults to an empty dict if not provided.

methods.name: humemai.memory.Memory.__repr__
documentation:
Provides a string representation of the Memory instance.

Returns:
    str: A formatted string representing the Memory instance.

methods.name: humemai.memory.Memory.to_dict
documentation:
Converts the Memory instance to a dictionary format.
Useful for serializing or passing data in a structured format.

Returns:
    dict[str, dict[str, any]]: A dictionary containing the head, tail, and edge data.
A Memory represents a relationship between two nodes with an edge connecting them.

classes.name: humemai.memory.ShortMemory

methods.name: humemai.memory.ShortMemory.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a ShortMemory instance.

Args:
    head_label (str): Label for the head node.
    tail_label (str): Label for the tail node.
    edge_label (str): Label for the edge between the head and tail nodes.
    head_properties (dict[str, any], optional): Properties for the head node.
        Defaults to an empty dict if not provided.
    tail_properties (dict[str, any], optional): Properties for the tail node.
        Defaults to an empty dict if not provided.
    edge_properties (dict[str, any]): Properties for the edge, including a required
        'current_time' field. Defaults to an empty dict if not provided.

methods.name: humemai.memory.ShortMemory.__repr__
documentation:
Provides a string representation of the ShortMemory instance.

Returns:
    str: A formatted string representing the ShortMemory instance.

A ShortMemory represents a Memory where the edge properties must include
`current_time`, if not provided, it will be added with the current time.

classes.name: humemai.memory.LongMemory

methods.name: humemai.memory.LongMemory.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a LongMemory instance.

Args:
    head_label (str): Label for the head node.
    tail_label (str): Label for the tail node.
    edge_label (str): Label for the edge between the head and tail nodes.
    head_properties (dict[str, any], optional): Properties for the head node.
        Defaults to an empty dict if not provided.
    tail_properties (dict[str, any], optional): Properties for the tail node.
        Defaults to an empty dict if not provided.
    edge_properties (dict[str, any], optional): Properties for the edge.
        Defaults to an empty dict if not provided.

methods.name: humemai.memory.LongMemory.__repr__
documentation:
Provides a string representation of the LongMemory instance.

Returns:
    str: A formatted string representing the LongMemory instance.

A LongMemory represents a long-term memory.

As soon as this memory is created, an edge property `recalled` is added with
value 0.

classes.name: humemai.memory.EpisodicMemory

methods.name: humemai.memory.EpisodicMemory.__init__
documentation:
Initializes an EpisodicMemory instance.

Args:
    head_label (str): Label for the head node.
    tail_label (str): Label for the tail node.
    edge_label (str): Label for the edge between the head and tail nodes.
    head_properties (dict[str, any], optional): Properties for the head node.
        Defaults to an empty dict if not provided.
    tail_properties (dict[str, any], optional): Properties for the tail node.
        Defaults to an empty dict if not provided.
    edge_properties (dict[str, any], optional): Properties for the edge.
        `event_time` is a required field.

methods.name: humemai.memory.EpisodicMemory.__repr__
documentation:
Provides a string representation of the EpisodicMemory instance.

Returns:
    str: A formatted string representing the EpisodicMemory instance.
A subclass of LongMemory that represents episodic memories.

classes.name: humemai.memory.SemanticMemory

methods.name: humemai.memory.SemanticMemory.__init__
documentation:
Initializes a SemanticMemory instance.

Args:
    head_label (str): Label for the head node.
    tail_label (str): Label for the tail node.
    edge_label (str): Label for the edge between the head and tail nodes.
    head_properties (dict[str, any], optional): Properties for the head node.
        Defaults to an empty dict if not provided.
    tail_properties (dict[str, any], optional): Properties for the tail node.
        Defaults to an empty dict if not provided.
    edge_properties (dict[str, any], optional): Properties for the edge.
        `known_since` is a required field.
        `derived_from` is a required field.

methods.name: humemai.memory.SemanticMemory.__repr__
documentation:
Provides a string representation of the SemanticMemory instance.

Returns:
    str: A formatted string representing the SemanticMemory instance.
A subclass of LongMemory that represents semantic memories.

modules.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin
documentation: Utility functions for interacting with JanusGraph using Gremlin Python.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.remove_all_data
documentation:
Remove all vertices and edges from the graph.

Args:
    g (Graph): JanusGraph graph instance.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.get_all_vertices
documentation:
Retrieve all vertices from the graph.

Args:
    g (Graph): JanusGraph graph instance.

Returns:
    list of Vertex: List of all vertices in the graph.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.get_all_edges
documentation:
Retrieve all edges from the graph.

Args:
    g (Graph): JanusGraph graph instance.

Returns:
    list of Edge: List of all edges in the graph.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.find_vertex_by_label
documentation:
Find a vertex by its label. It can return multiple vertices.

Args:
    g (Graph): JanusGraph graph instance.
    label (str): Label of the vertex.

Returns:
    list of Vertex: List of vertices with the provided label.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.find_vertices_by_properties
documentation:
Find vertices based on included and excluded properties.

Args:
    g (Graph): JanusGraph graph instance.
    include_keys (list of str): List of properties that must be included.
    exclude_keys (list of str, optional): List of properties that must be excluded.

Returns:
    list of Vertex: List of vertices matching the criteria.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.remove_vertex
documentation:
Removes a given vertex from the graph.

Args:
    g (GraphTraversalSource): The Gremlin graph traversal source.
    vertex (Vertex): The vertex to remove.

Returns:
    None

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.remove_edge
documentation:
Removes a given edge from the graph.

The syntax for removing an edge is different from removing a vertex. It's quite
annoying, but it is what it is.

Args:
    g (GraphTraversalSource): The Gremlin graph traversal source.
    edge (Edge): The edge to remove.

Returns:
    None

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.find_edge_by_vertices_and_label
documentation:
Find an edge by its label and property.

Args:
    g (Graph): JanusGraph graph instance.
    head (Vertex): Head vertex of the edge.
    label (str): Label of the edge.
    tail (Vertex): Tail vertex of the edge.

Returns:
    list of Edge: List of edges with the provided label.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.find_edge_by_label
documentation:
Find an edge by its label.

Args:
    g (Graph): JanusGraph graph instance.
    label (str): Label of the edge.

Returns:
    list of Edge: List of edges with the provided label.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.find_edges_by_properties
documentation:
Find edges based on included and excluded properties.

Args:
    g (Graph): JanusGraph graph instance.
    include_keys (list of str): List of properties that must be included.
    exclude_keys (list of str, optional): List of properties that must be excluded.

Returns:
    list of Edge: List of edges matching the criteria.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.create_vertex
documentation:
Create a vertex with the given properties.

Note that this does not check if the vertex already exists.

Args:
    g (Graph): JanusGraph graph instance.
    label (str): Label of the vertex.
    properties (dict): Dictionary of properties for the vertex.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.create_edge
documentation:
Create an edge between two vertices.

Note that this does not check if the edge already exists.

Args:
    g (Graph): JanusGraph graph instance.
    head (Vertex): Vertex where the edge originates.
    label (str): Label of the edge.
    tail (Vertex): Vertex where the edge terminates.
    properties (dict): Dictionary of properties for the edge.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.update_vertex_properties
documentation:
Update the properties of an existing vertex and return the updated vertex.

Args:
    g (Graph): JanusGraph graph instance.
    vertex (Vertex): Vertex to update.
    properties (dict): Dictionary of properties to update.

Returns:
    Vertex: The updated vertex.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.remove_vertex_properties
documentation:
Remove specific properties from an existing vertex and return the updated vertex.

Args:
    g (Graph): JanusGraph graph instance.
    vertex (Vertex): Vertex to update.
    property_keys (list): List of property keys to remove.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.update_edge_properties
documentation:
Update the properties of an existing edge and return the updated edge.

Args:
    g (Graph): JanusGraph graph instance.
    edge (Edge): Edge to update.
    properties (dict): Dictionary of properties to update.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.remove_edge_properties
documentation:
Remove specific properties from an existing edge and return the updated edge.

Args:
    g (Graph): JanusGraph graph instance.
    edge (Edge): Edge whose properties are to be removed.
    property_keys (list): List of property keys to remove.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.get_properties
documentation:
Retrieve all properties of a vertex or edge, decoding JSON-encoded values.

Args:
    vertex_or_edge (Vertex | Edge): Vertex or edge to retrieve properties for.

Returns:
    dict: Dictionary of properties for the element.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.get_vertices_within_hops
documentation:
Retrieve all vertices within N hops from a starting vertex.

Args:
    g (Graph): JanusGraph graph instance.
    vertices (list[Vertex]): List of starting vertex IDs for the traversal.
    hops (int): Number of hops to traverse from the starting vertex.

Returns:
    list[Vertex]: List of vertices within N hops from the starting vertex.

functions.name: humemai.janusgraph.utils.gremlin.get_edges_between_vertices
documentation:
Retrieve all edges between a list of vertices.

Args:
    g (Graph): JanusGraph graph instance.
    vertices (list[Vertex]): List of vertices to find edges between.

Returns:
    list[Edge]: List of edges between the provided vertices.

modules.name: humemai.prompt.prompt
documentation: Propmt specific functions and classes.

functions.name: humemai.prompt.prompt.get_hf_pipeline
documentation:
Get a text generation pipeline with the specified device and quantization.

Args:
    model (str): The model to use for text generation.
        Defaults to "meta-llama/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct".
        meta-llama/Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct,
        meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
        ...

        are also available.
    device (str): The device to run the pipeline on. Should be either "cuda" or
        "cpu".Defaults to "cpu".
    quantization (str): The quantization to apply to the model. Defaults to "16bit".

Returns:
    transformers.Pipeline: The text generation pipeline.

functions.name: humemai.prompt.prompt.text2graph
documentation:
Generate the prompt for the AI assistant to convert text to a simplified knowledge graph.

Args:
    memory (dict): The memory of the knowledge graph (history) extracted so far.
    next_text (str): The new text to convert into a knowledge graph.
    template (str): The template for the text-to-graph conversion. Currently it can
        be either "text2graph_without_properties" or "text2graph_with_properties".


Returns:
    list[dict]: A structured prompt for the AI assistant to build a knowledge graph.

functions.name: humemai.prompt.prompt.graph2text
documentation:
Generate the prompt for the AI assistant to convert a knowledge graph into text.

Args:
    memory (dict): The knowledge graph to convert into text.
    template (str): The template for the graph-to-text conversion. Currently it can
        be either "graph2text_without_properties" or "graph2text_with_properties

Returns:
    list[dict]: A structured prompt for the AI assistant to generate natural
    language text in JSON format.

modules.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai
documentation: Humemai class

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a Humemai object for connecting to JanusGraph and Cassandra
containers.

Args:
    cassandra_container_name (str): Name of the Cassandra container.
    janusgraph_container_name (str): Name of the JanusGraph container.
    gremlin_server_url (str): URL for connecting to the Gremlin server.
    gremlin_traversal_source (str): Traversal source name for Gremlin.
    cassandra_data_dir (str): Directory for Cassandra data persistence.
    configs_dir (str): Directory containing JanusGraph and Gremlin Server
    configuration files.
    janusgraph_config (str): JanusGraph configuration file.
    gremlin_server_config (str): Gremlin Server configuration file.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.start_containers
documentation:
Start the Cassandra and JanusGraph containers with optional warmup time.

Args:
    warmup_seconds (int): Number of seconds to wait after starting the
        containers

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.stop_containers
documentation: Stop the Cassandra and JanusGraph containers.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.remove_containers
documentation: Remove the Cassandra and JanusGraph containers.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.connect
documentation: Establish a connection to the Gremlin server.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.disconnect
documentation: Close the connection to the Gremlin server.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.remove_all_data
documentation: Remove all vertices and edges from the JanusGraph graph.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.write_short_term_vertex
documentation:
Write a new short-term vertex to the graph.

if 'current_time' property does not exist in 'properties', it will be added.

Args:
    label (str): Label of the vertex.
    properties (dict): Properties of the vertex.

Returns:
    Vertex: The newly created vertex.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.write_short_term_edge
documentation:
Write a new short-term edge to the graph.

if 'current_time' property does not exist in 'properties', it will be added.

Args:
    head_vertex (Vertex): Head vertex of the edge.
    edge_label (str): Label of the edge.
    tail_vertex (Vertex): Tail vertex of the edge.
    properties (dict): Properties of the edge.

Returns:
    Edge: The newly created edge.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.move_short_term_vertex
documentation:
Move the short-term vertex to another memory type.

Args:
    vertex (Vertex): The vertex to be moved.
    action (str): The action to be taken. Choose from 'episodic' or 'semantic'

Returns:
    Vertex: The updated vertex

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.move_short_term_edge
documentation:
Move the short-term edge to another memory type.

Args:
    edge (Edge): The edge to be moved.
    action (str): The action to be taken. Choose from 'episodic' or 'semantic'

Returns:
    Edge: The updated edge.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.remove_all_short_term
documentation:
Remove all pure short-term vertices and edges.

This method removes all vertices and edges that have 'current_time' property,
but not 'num_recalled', 'event_time', or 'known_since' properties, meaning that
they are pure short-term memories. This will also remove the 'current_time'
property from all the vertices and edges. Call this after you are done with
moving short-term memories to other memory types.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.write_long_term_vertex
documentation:
Write a new long-term vertex to the graph.

This is directly writing a vertex to the long-term memory, so the vertex must
should include the time information (e.g., 'event_time' or 'known_since'). One
thing to note is that the Vertex might already exist in the long-term memory.
In this case, the properties will be updated. You should add 'num_recalled=0'
property!

Args:
    label (str): Label of the vertex.
    properties (dict): Properties of the vertex.

Returns:
    Vertex: The updated vertex.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.write_long_term_edge
documentation:
Write a new long-term edge to the graph.

This is directly writing an edge to the long-term memory, so the edge must
should include the time information (e.g., 'event_time' or 'known_since'). One
thing to note is that the Edge might already exist in the long-term memory.
In this case, the properties will be updated. If not, a new edge will be
created, with 'num_recalled=0' property added.

Args:
    head_vertex (Vertex): Head vertex of the edge.
    edge_label (str): Label of the edge.
    tail_vertex (Vertex): Tail vertex of the edge.
    properties (dict): Properties of the edge.

Returns:
    Edge: The updated edge.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai._increment_num_recalled_vertices_and_edges
documentation:
Helper function to increment 'num_recalled' on vertices and edges

Args:
    vertices (list of Vertex): List of vertices to be updated.
    edges (list of Edge): List of edges to be updated.

Returns:
    tuple: A tuple of updated vertices and edges.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.get_working_vertices_and_edges
documentation:
Retrieves the working memory based on the short-term memories.

The short-term memories are used as a trigger to retrieve the working memory.

Args:
    short_term_vertices (list of Vertex): List of short-term vertices.
    short_term_edges (list of Edge): List of short-term edges.
    include_all_long_term (bool): If True, include all long-term memories.
    hops (int): Number of hops to traverse from the trigger node.

Returns:
    tuple: A tuple of short-term vertices, long-term vertices, short-term edges,
        and long-term edges.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.get_all_vertices
documentation:
Retrieve all vertices from the graph.

Returns:
    list of Vertex: List of all vertices.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.get_all_edges
documentation:
Retrieve all edges from the graph.

Returns:
    list of Edge: List of all edges.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.get_all_short_term_vertices
documentation:
Retrieve all short-term vertices from the graph.

Returns:
    list of Vertex: List of short-term vertices.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.get_all_short_term_edges
documentation:
Retrieve all short-term edges from the graph.

Returns:
    list of Edge: List of short-term edges.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.get_all_long_term_vertices
documentation:
Retrieve all long-term vertices from the graph.

Returns:
    list of Vertex: List of long-term vertices.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.get_all_long_term_edges
documentation:
Retrieve all long-term edges from the graph.

Returns:
    list of Edge: List of long-term edges.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.get_all_episodic_vertices
documentation:
Retrieve all episodic vertices from the graph.

Returns:
    list of Vertex: List of episodic vertices.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.get_all_episodic_edges
documentation:
Retrieve all episodic edges from the graph.

Returns:
    list of Edge: List of episodic edges.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.get_all_semantic_vertices
documentation:
Retrieve all semantic vertices from the graph.

Returns:
    list of Vertex: List of semantic vertices.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.get_all_semantic_edges
documentation:
Retrieve all semantic edges from the graph.

Returns:
    list of Edge: List of semantic edges.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.get_vertex_properties
documentation:
Retrieve the properties of a vertex.

Args:
    vertex (Vertex): The vertex to retrieve properties from.

Returns:
    dict: The properties of the vertex.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.get_edge_properties
documentation:
Retrieve the properties of an edge.

Args:
    edge (Edge): The edge to retrieve properties from.

Returns:
    dict: The properties of the edge.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.remove_vertex
documentation:
Remove a vertex from the graph.

Args:
    vertex (Vertex): The vertex to be removed.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.remove_edge
documentation:
Remove an edge from the graph.

Args:
    edge (Edge): The edge to be removed.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.find_vertex_by_label
documentation:
Find vertices by label.

Args:
    label (str): The label to search for.

Returns:
    list of Vertex: List of vertices with the given label.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.save_db_as_json
documentation:
Read the database as a JSON file.

Args:
    json_name (str): The name of the JSON file.

methods.name: humemai.janusgraph.humemai.Humemai.load_db_from_json
documentation:
Write a JSON file to the database.

Args:
    json_name (str): The name of the JSON file.

modules.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai
documentation: Humemai Class with RDFLib

classes.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.add_memory
documentation:
Add a reified statement to the RDF graph, with the main triple and optional
qualifiers. Ensure that everything is in the correct URIRef format.

Args:
    triples (list): A list of triples (subject, predicate, object) to be added.
    qualifiers (dict): A dictionary of qualifiers (e.g., location, currentTime).

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.delete_memory
documentation:
Delete a memory (reified statement) by its unique ID, including all associated
qualifiers.

Args:
    memory_id (Literal): The unique ID of the memory to be deleted.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.get_memory_by_id
documentation:
Retrieve a memory (reified statement) by its unique ID and return its details.

Args:
    memory_id (Literal): The unique ID of the memory to retrieve.

Returns:
    dict: A dictionary with the memory details (subject, predicate, object,
    qualifiers).

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.delete_triple
documentation:
Delete a triple from the RDF graph, including all of its qualifiers.

Args:
    subject (URIRef): The subject of the memory triple.
    predicate (URIRef): The predicate of the memory triple.
    object_ (URIRef): The object of the memory triple.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.add_short_term_memory
documentation:
Add short-term memories to the RDF graph, enforcing required qualifiers.

Args:
    triples (list): A list of triples to add.
    qualifiers (dict, optional): Additional qualifiers to add.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.add_episodic_memory
documentation:
Add episodic memories to the RDF graph, enforcing required qualifiers.

Args:
    triples (list): A list of triples to add.
    qualifiers (dict, optional): Additional qualifiers to add.
        The qualifiers can have the following in URIRef format:
        https://humem.ai/ontology#eventTime: str,
        https://humem.ai/ontology#location: str,
        https://humem.ai/ontology#emotion: str,
        https://humem.ai/ontology#event: str,
    event_properties (dict, optional): Additional properties for the event node.
        The properties should be URIRef format.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.add_event
documentation:
Create an event node in the RDF graph with custom properties.

Args:
    event (URIRef): The URIRef of the event.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.add_event_properties
documentation:
Add properties to an existing event node in the RDF graph.

Args:
    event (URIRef): The URIRef of the event.
    event_properties (dict): Additional properties for the event node.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.add_semantic_memory
documentation:
Add semantic memories to the RDF graph, enforcing required qualifiers.

Args:
    triples (list): A list of triples to add.
    qualifiers (dict, optional): Additional qualifiers to add.
        The qualifiers can have the following:
        https://humem.ai/ontology#knownSince: str,
        https://humem.ai/ontology#derivedFrom: str,
        https://humem.ai/ontology#strength: int,

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.get_memories
documentation:
Retrieve memories with optional filtering based on the qualifiers and triple
values, including time bounds.

Args:
    subject (URIRef, optional): Filter by subject URI.
    predicate (URIRef, optional): Filter by predicate URI.
    object_ (URIRef, optional): Filter by object URI.
    qualifiers (dict, optional): Additional qualifiers to filter by.
    lower_time_bound (Literal, optional): Lower bound for time filtering (ISO format).
    upper_time_bound (Literal, optional): Upper bound for time filtering (ISO format).

Returns:
    Memory: A new Memory object containing the filtered memories.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.get_raw_triple_count
documentation:
Count the number of raw triples in the RDF graph.

Returns:
    int: The count of raw triples.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.get_main_triple_count_except_event
documentation:
Count the number of unique memories (subject-predicate-object triples) in the
graph. This does not count reified statements.

Returns:
    int: The count of unique memories.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.get_memory_count
documentation:
Count the number of reified statements (RDF statements) in the graph.
This counts the reified statements instead of just the main triples.

Returns:
    int: The count of reified statements.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.get_short_term_memory_count
documentation:
Count the number of short-term memories in the graph.
Short-term memories are reified statements that have the 'currentTime'
qualifier.

Returns:
    int: The count of short-term memories.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.get_long_term_episodic_memory_count
documentation:
Count the number of long-term episodic memories in the graph.
Long-term episodic memories are reified statements that have the 'eventTime'
qualifier.

Returns:
    int: The count of long-term episodic memories.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.get_long_term_semantic_memory_count
documentation:
Count the number of long-term semantic memories in the graph.
Long-term semantic memories are reified statements that have the 'knownSince'
qualifier.

Returns:
    int: The count of long-term semantic memories.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.get_long_term_memory_count
documentation:
Count the number of long-term memories in the graph.
Long-term memories are reified statements that do NOT have the 'currentTime'
qualifier.

Returns:
    int: The count of long-term memories.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.get_event_count
documentation:
Count the number of Event instances in the RDF graph.

Returns:
    int: The number of Event instances.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.modify_strength
documentation:
Modify the strength of long-term memories by incrementing/decrementing or
multiplying.

Args:
    filters (dict): Filters to identify the memory, including subject,
        predicate, object, and any qualifiers.
    increment_by (int, optional): Increment or decrement the strength value by
        this amount.
    multiply_by (float, optional): Multiply the strength value by this factor.
        Rounded to the nearest integer.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.modify_episodic_event
documentation:
Modify the event value for episodic memories that fall within a specific time
range and optional filters.

Args:
    upper_time_bound (Literal): The upper bound for time filtering (ISO format).
    lower_time_bound (Literal): The lower bound for time filtering (ISO format).
    new_event (URIRef): The new value for the event qualifier.
    subject (URIRef, optional): Filter by subject URI.
    predicate (URIRef, optional): Filter by predicate URI.
    object_ (URIRef, optional): Filter by object URI.
    qualifiers (dict, optional): Additional qualifiers to filter by.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.increment_recalled
documentation:
Increment the 'recalled' value for memories (episodic or semantic) that match
the filters.

Args:
    subject (URIRef, optional): Filter by subject URI.
    predicate (URIRef, optional): Filter by predicate URI.
    object_ (URIRef, optional): Filter by object URI.
    qualifiers (dict, optional): Additional qualifiers to filter by.
    lower_time_bound (Literal, optional): Lower bound for time filtering (ISO format).
    upper_time_bound (Literal, optional): Upper bound for time filtering (ISO format).

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai._strip_namespace
documentation:
Helper function to strip the namespace and return the last part of a URIRef.

Args:
    uri (URIRef or Literal): The URIRef to process.

Returns:
    str: The last part of the URI after the last '/' or '#'.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.is_reified_statement_short_term
documentation:
Check if a given reified statement is a short-term memory by verifying if it
has a 'currentTime' qualifier.

Args:
    statement (URIRef): The reified statement to check.

Returns:
    bool: True if it's a short-term memory, False otherwise.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai._add_reified_statement_to_working_memory_and_increment_recall
documentation:
Helper method to add all reified statements (including qualifiers) of a given triple
to the working memory and increment the recall count for each reified statement.

Args:
    subj (URIRef): Subject of the triple.
    pred (URIRef): Predicate of the triple.
    obj (URIRef): Object of the triple.
    working_memory (Memory): The working memory to which the statements and qualifiers are added.
    specific_statement (URIRef, optional): A specific reified statement to process, if provided.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.get_short_term_memories
documentation:
Query the RDF graph to retrieve all short-term memories with a currentTime
qualifier and include all associated qualifiers (e.g., location, emotion, etc.).

Returns:
    Memory: A Memory object containing all short-term memories with their qualifiers.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.get_long_term_memories
documentation:
Retrieve all long-term memories from the RDF graph.
Long-term memories are identified by the presence of either 'eventTime' or 'knownSince'
qualifiers and the absence of a 'currentTime' qualifier.

Returns:
    Memory: A new Memory object containing all long-term memories (episodic and
    semantic).

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.load_from_ttl
documentation:
Load memory data from a Turtle (.ttl) file into the RDF graph.

Args:
    ttl_file (str): Path to the Turtle file to load.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.save_to_ttl
documentation:
Save the current memory graph to a Turtle (.ttl) file.

Args:
    ttl_file (str): Path to the Turtle file to save.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.iterate_memories
documentation:
Iterate over memories in the graph, filtered by memory type (short-term,
long-term, episodic, semantic, or all).

Args:
    memory_type (str, optional): The type of memory to iterate over.
        Valid values: "short_term", "long_term", "episodic", "semantic", or "all".
        - "short_term": Short-term memories (with 'currentTime').
        - "long_term": Long-term memories (with 'eventTime' or 'knownSince', but without 'currentTime').
        - "episodic": Long-term episodic memories (with 'eventTime', but without 'knownSince' and 'currentTime').
        - "semantic": Long-term semantic memories (with 'knownSince', but without 'eventTime' and 'currentTime').
        - "all": Iterate over all memories (both short-term and long-term).
        If None, defaults to "all".

Yields:
    tuple: (subject, predicate, object, qualifiers) for each memory that matches
    the criteria.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.iterate_events
documentation:
Iterate over all event nodes in the RDF graph.

Yields:
    URIRef: Each event node that is an instance of humemai:Event.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.print_events
documentation:
Retrieve all triples where an Event entity is involved either as the subject or the object
and format them as a readable string.

Args:
    debug (bool): If True, return the string instead of printing.

Returns:
    str: A formatted string containing all event-related triples.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.print_all_raw_triples
documentation:
Print all triples in the graph in a readable format.

Args:
    debug (bool): If True, return the string instead of printing.

Returns:
    str: A formatted string of all triples.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.print_memories
documentation:
Print all memories in the graph in a readable format.

Args:
    debug (bool): If True, return the string instead of printing.

Returns:
    str: A formatted string of all memories.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.get_working_memory
documentation:
Retrieve working memory based on a trigger node and a specified number of hops.
It fetches all triples within N hops from the trigger node in the long-term
memory, including their qualifiers. Also includes all short-term memories.

If `include_all_long_term` is True, all long-term memories will be included,
regardless of the BFS traversal or hops.

For each long-term memory retrieved, the 'recalled' qualifier is incremented by 1.

Args:
    trigger_node (URIRef, optional): The starting node for memory traversal.
    hops (int, optional): The number of hops for BFS traversal (default: 0).
    include_all_long_term (bool, optional): Include all long-term memories
    (default: False).

Returns:
    Memory: A new Memory object containing the working memory (short-term +
    relevant long-term memories).

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.move_short_term_to_episodic
documentation:
Move the specified short-term memory to long-term episodic memory.

Args:
    memory_id_to_move (Literal): The memory ID to move from short-term to long-term.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.move_short_term_to_semantic
documentation:
Move the specified short-term memory to long-term semantic memory.

Args:
    memory_id_to_move (Literal): The memory ID to move from short-term to long-term.

methods.name: humemai.rdflib.humemai.Humemai.clear_short_term_memories
documentation: Clear all short-term memories from the memory system.

Memory class for managing both short-term and long-term memories.
Provides methods to add, retrieve, delete, cluster, and manage memories in the RDF graph.
